UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the AI Coach Connect application, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalId.description: Unique identifier for the user from the external authentication system. (Relationship: ExternalAuth 1:1 UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

CoachingSession.title: CoachingSession
CoachingSession.description: Represents a single coaching session between a user and the AI coach.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CoachingSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who participated in this session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CoachingSession)

startTime.description: The date and time when the coaching session began.

endTime.description: The date and time when the coaching session ended.

durationMinutes.description: The total duration of the coaching session in minutes.

simliSessionId.description: Optional identifier provided by the Simli platform for this specific session, if available.

geminiSessionId.description: Optional identifier provided by the Gemini platform for this specific session, if available.

transcriptSummary.description: A summarized version of the conversation transcript from the session.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the coaching session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the coaching session record was last updated.

SessionFeedback.title: SessionFeedback
SessionFeedback.description: Represents feedback provided by a user for a specific coaching session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SessionFeedback entity.

sessionId.description: Reference to the CoachingSession this feedback is for. (Relationship: CoachingSession 1:N SessionFeedback)

rating.description: A numerical rating provided by the user for the session (e.g., 1-5).

comments.description: Detailed textual comments provided by the user about the session.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the feedback was submitted.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) must explicitly match the user's `request.auth.uid` (UserProfile.externalId) for direct path-based authorization. No denormalization for authorization is needed as access is determined by the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid` and `UserProfile.externalId`.

definition.description: Stores individual coaching session records for a specific user. The `userId` field within the document must match the `userId` in the path for data integrity, and access is restricted to the owning user via path-based authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this session, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the coaching session.

definition.description: Stores feedback for a specific coaching session. To ensure authorization independence and simplify security rules, each feedback document will include a denormalized `userId` field, matching the `userId` in the path and the owning user's ID. This avoids the need for `get()` calls to parent documents for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the session and provided the feedback, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the coaching session this feedback pertains to.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for the session feedback.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling. This design ensures that security rules are simple, robust, and easy to debug.

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection `users`. Each document in this collection represents a `UserProfile`, with its document ID (`userId`) explicitly matching the user's `externalId` (which corresponds to `request.auth.uid`). This path-based ownership makes authorization for a user's own profile direct and atomic.

2.  **Coaching Sessions (`/users/{userId}/coaching_sessions/{sessionId}`):** Coaching sessions are stored as a subcollection under the specific user's profile. This hierarchical structure immediately ties each `CoachingSession` to its `UserProfile` owner. The `CoachingSession` entity naturally includes a `userId` field, which will be validated against the `userId` in the document path (`request.auth.uid`). This provides an inherent ownership model, where a session is always owned by the user whose ID is in the parent path.

3.  **Session Feedback (`/users/{userId}/coaching_sessions/{sessionId}/feedback/{feedbackId}`):** Feedback for a coaching session is stored as a subcollection under the specific `CoachingSession` document. Crucially, the `SessionFeedback` entity, as defined, does not inherently contain a `userId` field. To uphold **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)**, each `SessionFeedback` document will be augmented to include a denormalized `userId` field. This `userId` will match the `userId` of the owning `UserProfile` and the `userId` in the parent path.

**How the structure achieves Authorization Independence:**
*   **`/users/{userId}`:** Access rules only need to check `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the document ID). No `get()` calls to parent documents are necessary.
*   **`/users/{userId}/coaching_sessions/{sessionId}`:** Access rules only need to check `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the path parameter). The `userId` field within the `CoachingSession` document serves as an integrity check, but authorization relies on the path. No `get()` calls to the parent `UserProfile` document are necessary.
*   **`/users/{userId}/coaching_sessions/{sessionId}/feedback/{feedbackId}`:** By denormalizing `userId` into each `SessionFeedback` document, authorization rules can directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`. This completely eliminates the need for `get()` calls to the parent `CoachingSession` or grandparent `UserProfile` documents to determine ownership, making rules atomic and independent.

**How the structure supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   **Structural Segregation and Path-Based Ownership:** All data within a given path segment (e.g., `/users/{userId}/coaching_sessions`) inherently belongs to the specified user (`userId`). When a user performs a `list` operation on a subcollection (e.g., `db.collection('users').doc(uid).collection('coaching_sessions')`), Firestore's security rules can grant read access if `request.auth.uid == userId`. Because the data is already segregated by user ID in the path, the `list` operation will only return documents owned by that user. No additional `where` clauses in the query are required to satisfy security rules, preventing accidental data exposure and ensuring queries are efficient. The `userId` denormalization in `SessionFeedback` further reinforces this, allowing `list` operations on feedback subcollections to be directly secured by `resource.data.userId` without filtering on query results.
